News.title: Horrific moment thug kills dad with single punch after downing eight pints of Guinness and Tequila Rose at charity bash
News.content:
THIS is the horrific moment a thug killed a popular dad with a single punch after downing eight pints and shots at a charity event.

David Lewis attacked Jack Oliver Frogell, known as Olly, in a “vicious” and “unprovoked” assault in October last year.





Footage of the horror has now been released by Hertfordshire Police after Lewis, 38, was sentenced on Wednesday.

CCTV shows Olly arriving at a charity event at Hemel Hempstead Town FC to pick up his wife and kids.

He chats to pals with a drink before playing a game of pool with a young lad.

As Olly goes outside, alcohol-fuelled Lewis follows and approaches from behind.

He taps the dad-of-two on the shoulder and in a matter of seconds knocks him to the ground.

The selfish thug walks away and leaves the scene without checking on his unconscious victim.

Olly was later rushed to hospital with a fractured skull and traumatic brain injury.



He tragically died in hospital 10 days later when the decision was made to turn off his life support.

Prosecutor Jonathan Mole told Luton Crown Court: “On the evening of October 24, the defendant attended a party fundraiser in aid of Breast Cancer Awareness.

“Mr Frogell entered the bar area and spoke to a group of other regulars from the football club, before playing a game of pool with a young male.”

Mr Mole told the court there had been a “disturbance” as the event was ending, which involved a friend of Lewis’.

The prosecutor said Oliver’s manner was described by witnesses as “casual and not aggressive”, the court heard.



“The defendant gestured in his direction and walked after Mr Frogell. The two had a brief interaction, with Mr Frogell then walking away,” said Mr Mole.

“[Lewis] followed him further up the road and attempted to tap him on the shoulder.



“After a very brief interaction – no longer than five seconds – the defendant delivered a single punch to Mr Frogell’s face.

“Mr Frogell fell to the ground. The defendant walked away from the football club, leaving Mr Frogell unconscious on the pavement.”

Married dad-of-two Lewis voluntarily went to a police station the day after the incident after hearing the victim was in a coma.

But he told officers the punch was thrown in “pre-emptive self-defence”.

Defending, John Ryder KC told the court Lewis had shown “genuine regret and remorse”, calling him a “decent man”.



Lewis, of Hemel Hempstead, admitted manslaughter and was jailed for four years and two months.

Upon sentencing, Judge Michael Simon said the defendant’s “alcohol-fuelled misjudgment” was the reason for Oliver’s death.

He said: “The night of October 24, 2025, was meant to be one of charity and community support, but instead it became one of tragedy and communal mourning.

“Olly was 48 years old. His sudden death in such tragic circumstances came as such a shock to family, friends and the wider community.”

The judge added: “It was at best a monumental, alcohol-fuelled misjudgement with catastrophic consequences.



“You didn’t stay to see what outcome of your violence was.”

In harrowing victim impact statements, Oliver’s family also paid heartbreaking tributes to the beloved father.

Devastated son, Freddie, said: “You have taken everything from me.

“I will never get back my dad. I don’t understand how any man can cause such hurt to my gentle dad.

“The year that Arsenal are actually going to win the league and he’s not going to see it.



“A part of me died that day, as well. [My dad] will never be there to see me get married or meet my kids.

“With one unprovoked punch, you have taken my world from me.”

Oliver’s wife, Lisa Frogell-Mason, said in an emotional statement: “Life for us will never be the same again. I have been with Olly since 1999.

“We got engaged and had two beautiful children. We had so much to look forward to.

“That night destroyed us as a family. The incident took place at a charity event we’ve attended for the last six years.



“This has been scarred by this horrendous act of violence.

“You decided to destroy our future. Rather than help him, you walked away. You left his lifeless body on the floor.

“It’s inhumane what you did. I will never forgive you as long as I live.

“I hope that haunts you for the rest of your life, because it does ours.

“I ran out with our children to something I can only describe as a horror scene: my husband, their father, fighting for his life.



“I knew he was gone. Having to keep our children back and telling them they couldn’t be with their dad was one of the hardest things I have ever had to do.

“I had to pull my son off his dad. He didn’t want to let him go.

“It absolutely rips me apart. To hear the sheer force of the punch caused the brain damage.

“Financially, you have ruined us as a family. I will never forgive you for how you ruined us as a family.

“[Olly] will never be able to walk his daughter down the aisle. We won’t get to grow old together.”



Oliver’s disabled father, Jack Frogell, called Lewis “evil” and said: “Apart from losing my son, I have also lost my best mate. Words can’t describe how this has made me feel.

“Olly was taken from me in a senseless, unprovoked, vicious assault by an evil, violent thug who has ruined my life.

“The little time I have left, I have been deprived by a thug who cowardly ran away after the unprovoked attack and left Olly fighting for his life.”

Brother Jay Frogell said he could no longer watch football after the tragedy.

He said: “All football is now a reminder to me that Olly is no longer with us.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Olly’s needless and senseless death has had a massive impact on me and my family. Nothing that happens today can change that.”

Detective Inspector Caeva Taylor, from the Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire and Hertfordshire Major Crime Unit, said: “This has been a heartbreaking ordeal for Olly’s family and friends, and our thoughts are with them as they continue to deal with their loss.”

News.title: Russian alcoholics ‘rounded up’ from rehab centre, robbed & sent to Ukraine as troops rage of ‘cripples who can’t walk’
News.content:
RUSSIA is sending alcoholics from across the country to wage Putin’s war against Ukraine.

In recent video footage, a Russian soldier claimed new arrivals to his unit were older, physically unfit and dealing with the effects of alcohol dependency.





The unnamed serviceman said the men were rounded up by “black recruiters” at a facility in Petrozavodsk and shipped to the front line.

These “black recruiters” refer to intermediaries who enlist men for military service through coercion, deception, or outright fraud.

Operating alongside or in the shadow of the official recruitment system, they are often paid bonuses for enlisting men, regardless of their condition.

Once these booze-addled recruits were gathered together, the mysterious men confiscated their credit cards, draining their bank accounts of all their contents.

In an expletive-laden rant, the soldier said: “They f*****g recruited a bunch of cripples who can’t even f*****g walk on top of being mentally unwell.

“I saw it myself – from Petrozavodsk, from Karelia, they recruited these f*****g guys from rehab, f*****g drunks.”

“What’s up with that guy? How does he managed to get around?” the soldier added.

“Even if he does make it there, he won’t even be able to [fight], you know?”



These are the latest claims that Russia has relied on questionable methods to recruit soldiers who are either physically or mentally unfit for combat.

Among those targeted are men with serious illnesses, mental disabilities or foreigners who have been allegedly pressured into joining up.

Ukrainian officials say this highlights how Moscow is increasingly dependent on vulnerable recruits.

According to the Ukrainian Security and Cooperation Centre (USCC), the number of Russians killed in Ukraine in January exceeded the number of new recruits by approximately 9,000.

Oksana Kuzan, co-founder and head of the analytical department of the USCC, said: “Given the situation, Russia has indeed been actively recruiting vulnerable groups, including people with various addictions, the unemployed, the ill, and former prisoners, and has also been using different methods to recruit migrants from other countries into its armed forces.”



She added Russian propagandists have indicated that the Kremlin has set specific recruitment quotas at a regional level.

However, local officials will often send almost anyone to the front line with little to no regard for suitability.

These men’s signatures are often forged, or they are given the unsavoury choice between military service or time behind bars in one of Russia‘s hellhole prisons.

To make matters worse, Russia is also accused of recruiting soldiers infected with horrible diseases including HIV and hepatitis.

These recruits are then reportedly deployed into segregated units and told to wear armbands identifying themselves.



On the front line, some soldiers are sent into the throes of battle with little to no protective equipment.

Terrifying video footage shows soldiers without helmets or armour.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

To replace the thousands of soldiers lost in mindless combat, Russia is now thought to have turned to Africa for its latest recruitment drive.

“It can be assumed that as Russian military losses in the war against Ukraine increase, and the number of contract soldiers declines, the activity of such ‘black recruiters’ will also intensify,” Kuzan said.

News.title: Gang-rape victim Noelia Castillo Ramos, 25, dies by euthanasia after grieving supporters gather and mum’s final goodbye
News.content:
PARALYSED gang-rape victim Noelia Castillo Ramos, 25, has today died by euthanasia after a heartbreaking farewell from her mum.

She is understood to have passed away around 20 minutes after receiving the first of three injections at a hospital near Barcelona.





The first two jabs induced deep sedation and the third most potent one caused cardiac arrest.

Noelia’s mum Yolanda Ramos spent last night with her at Sant Camil Hospital before the start of the scheduled 6pm local time procedure designed to “ensure a death without suffering”.

Her father Geronimo Castillo was among other relatives including her siblings and maternal grandmother who arrived at the hospital to spend time with her earlier today.



Before she was euthanised, the 25-year-old is said to have asked her family to spend extra time with her.

They were due to leave her alone with a medic who administered her three injections at around 5.30pm but are understood to have stayed for at least an extra half-hour.

The scheduled start of the procedure is believed to have begun around 6.30pm local time.

Geronimo waged a lengthy legal fight to try to get judges to block his daughter’s euthanasia request with the support of a Valladolid-based ultraconservative group called Christian Lawyers.



But Noelia, who spoke openly about her decision to end the mental and physical pain she has suffered for years during a Spanish TV interview earlier this week, said she wanted to “leave in peace”.

She was alone in the room when she died at her own request, apart from the doctor administering her injections.

In a Tuesday interview on Spain’s national Antena 3 station, Noelia admitted: “None of my family support me but the happiness of a father does not have to be above that of a daughter or above a daughter’s life.

“I just want to go in peace and stop suffering.”

Revealing how she wanted to leave this world, she added: “I’ve always thought I want to die beautiful.



“I’ll put on my prettiest dress and make-up.”

Supporters gathered today outside the Spanish hospital where Noelia’s life ended, in a last-ditch attempt to change her mind.

Her mother, Yolanda said: “I am not in favour of euthanasia, of course I am not in favour, but I will always be by her side until the very last moment, as long as she allows me.”

How to get help EVERY 90 minutes in the UK a life is lost to suicide. It doesn’t discriminate, touching the lives of people in every corner of society – from the homeless and unemployed to builders and doctors, reality stars and footballers. It’s the biggest killer of people under the age of 35, more deadly than cancer and car crashes. And men are three times more likely to take their own life than women. Yet it’s rarely spoken of, a taboo that threatens to continue its deadly rampage unless we all stop and take notice, now. If you, or anyone you know, needs help dealing with mental health problems, the following organisations provide support: CALM, www.thecalmzone.net, 0800 585 858

Heads Together,www.headstogether.org.uk

HUMEN www.wearehumen.org

Mind, www.mind.org.uk, 0300 123 3393

Papyrus, www.papyrus-uk.org, 0800 068 41 41

Samaritans,www.samaritans.org, 116 123

In a heartbreaking final interview, she revealed: “I’ve been praying and thinking… hoping that at the last moment she’ll say ‘I regret it’.”

The distraught mum gave one last plea for her daughter to change her mind, reading aloud offers of financial help to get her daughter new treatments.



One email read: “Perhaps with significant help, she could begin to rebuild her life little by little.”

Yolanda added: “These are my final words.

“There’s nothing more I can do. If you decide to go ahead, I’ll be there for you, but if you change your mind, I’ll be there for the good times too.”

Noelia’s life began derailing after her parents divorced when she was a child and she was already receiving psychiatric treatment as a 13-year-old.

She ended up in care and suffered two sexual assaults, the last one a gang rape by three boys in a nightclub.



The harrowing attack, which Noelia has admitted she didn’t report to police, led to her October 4, 2022 suicide attempt, leaving her with a serious and irreversible spinal cord injury.

She uses a wheelchair to get around and suffers from excruciating leg and back pain which she says makes sleep difficult.

Noelia has admitted to suffering a borderline personality disorder and another compulsive obsessive one.

The 25-year-old was “relying on the Spanish mental healthcare system” before she was raped, her family said.

Her condition was deemed “serious, chronic and disabling” under Spain‘s landmark 2021 euthanasia laws.



Noelia’s dad, supported by conservative advocacy group Abogados Cristianos (Christian Lawyers), argued that his daughter’s mental health was affecting her ability to make a free and informed decision.

But judges have insisted her mental faculties are “intact”, making her dramatic decision “free, conscious and informed” in line with the Spanish law on euthanasia.

Noelia eventually got the go ahead for her euthanasia from the European Court of Human Rights.



She said on Spanish TV earlier this week: “Before requesting euthanasia, I saw my world as very dark.”

“I had no goals or objectives.

“I have always felt alone, I have never felt understood, and no one has ever empathized with me.”



Questioning her dad’s decisions which delayed her euthanasia wish, she added: “He has not respected my decision and never will.”

Christian Lawyers, the organisation which has been representing Noelia’s dad, said in a statement released around 7pm UK time: “Noelia has already been euthanized.

“We deeply regret her death and denounce that this case highlights the serious flaws in the euthanasia law, which does not protect the most vulnerable people.

“Thanks to everyone who has empathized with the family during these very difficult times.

“You can understand that the parents are broken after years of trying to support her in her rehabilitation.”



But sympathiser Carla Gomez said: “Rest in peace and to those who don’t like Noelia’s decision, lump it.

“It’s her life and she has the right to decide.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

In June 2021, Spain became the fourth European Union country to legalise euthanasia and assisted suicide for people with incurable or severely debilitating conditions who wish to end their lives.

Spanish law states that applicants who are over 18-years-old with “chronic or invalidating suffering” can choose to receive assisted dying.

News.title: Invisible goblins made me shoot four people dead in bloody church gun rampage, former cop tells judge
News.content:
HIDDEN CREATURES Invisible goblins made me shoot four people dead in bloody church gun rampage, former cop tells judge

A FORMER detective who went berserk and shot four people dead has told a judge that goblins made him do it.

Jaison Muvevi claimed the goblins had been “tormenting” him and forced him to drive to a church shrine with his mum.





Then they ordered him to shoot 62-year-old local “prophet” Chrispen Kanerusine with his pistol, the High Court in the Zimbabwean capital of Harare heard.

He also allegedly killed local police inspector Maxwell Hove, barman Munashe Majani and another man called Nyarai Round, as well as wounding four more people.

Muvevi, 42, who was married to three women in different towns at the same time and had seven children, later fled to Mozambique.

Defence lawyer Jackie Sande said he was obviously “unhinged”.

She added: “State wit­nesses admit that on the date of the com­mis­sion of the offences, the accused was rest­less and was being haunted by gob­lins.

“That accused per­son, without any con­science, kicked the bod­ies of his vic­tims, par­tic­u­larly the officer shot by him.”

Muvevi denies murder. The trial continues.

Goblins often cause problems in Zimbabwe, but are said to be invisible to everybody except the person they attack.



Naked female goblins once chased terrified policemen out of the nick in the Matabeleland town of Bulilima.

And screaming schoolgirls fled a high school in Bulawayo after being attacked in their classroom by dwarf goblins who metamorphosised into baboons.

A woman called Priviledge Jozi told how she suffered four consecutive miscarriages after being “unknowingly” married off to a goblin, while Nyasha Tayerwa and her husband both claimed to have been forced to marry goblins.

Sad Nyasha moaned: “My husband and I can no longer have lula-lula together.”

Loveness Nota, 41, blamed a goblin curse for the fact three of her husbands died while the other two dumped her.



She complained: “Men now fear to approach me.

“Many young girls are dying in households where goblins are active. Women are sexually abused by invisible men. We urgently need help.”

Goblin interference was also suspected when Custom Mutinhima, the headman of Mutinhima Village in east Zimbabwe, suddenly collapsed and died.

Other relatives accused his brother Aaron of possessing “mubobobo”, a special charm that enabled him to “have spiritual sex at a distance with a person of his choice”.



But Aaron protested his innocence and said he had nothing to do with the goblins.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

He insisted: “I don’t know why I have been labelled a wizard. I just visited my brother when he was bedridden and then he died.”

The local leader, Chief Mutasa, ordered the family to stop their “shenanigans” and start a “cleansing process” immediately to get rid of the goblins.

News.title: Family pay heartbreaking tribute to ‘bright and beautiful’ teen girl killed in horror crash as boy, 17, arrested
News.content:
'LOVING GIRL' Family pay heartbreaking tribute to ‘bright and beautiful’ teen girl killed in horror crash as boy, 17, arrested

THE family of a 17-year-old girl killed in a fatal collision have paid tribute to her as cops appeal for witnesses.

The incident happened at around 9.05pm on Wednesday in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire.



One of the passengers, Megan Swann, died at the scene and two other teenage girls were taken to hospital with serious injuries.



The driver of the vehicle, a blue Ford Feista, a 17-year-old boy, was arrested on suspicion of causing death by dangerous driving and causing injury by dangerous driving.

In tribute to Megan, her family said: “Megan was a daughter, sister, niece, granddaughter, cousin and friend.

“She was a bright, beautiful, caring and loving young girl.

“She had a zest for life with an infectious personality.



“If you met her once you would never forget her. You would hear Megan before you saw her and hear her laughter.

“She was doing so well with her college and was on track to pass her hair and beauty course with flying colours.

“She was only 17 with her whole life ahead of her.

“Her sudden death has left us all with a deep ache that will never heal.



“She will be forever missed.

“Megan, we all love you xx”

Detective Sergeant Ben Heath from the Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire and Hertfordshire Serious Collision Investigation Unit, said: “Our thoughts are with Megan’s family at this difficult time.

“I am appealing for any information that could help with our enquiries. Did you see a blue Ford Fiesta travelling in the area at the time?

“Or did you witness the events leading up to the collision?



“If you were travelling in the area and have a dash cam fitted, please review your footage as you may have captured something which could assist our investigation.”

Anyone with information can email the Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire and Hertfordshire Serious Collision Investigation Unit.

You can also report information online, speak to an operator in our Force Communications Room via our online web chat or call the non-emergency number 101, quoting Op Douglas.

More to follow… For the latest news on this story keep checking back at The Sun Online

News.title: Lucy Letby cops used ‘flawed’ experts & may have broken law during investigation, MP blasts – as Sun probe is praised
News.content:
COPS who investigated Lucy Letby used “flawed” experts and may have broken the law, an MP has slammed.

In a damning speech, Tory MP Sir David Davis today said Cheshire Police ignored signs Letby’s neonatal unit was failing and “cherry-picked” statistics to use against her, and selected flawed experts.





He has called for a retrial on Letby’s convictions of murder and attempted murder.

The Sun’s work with the man who caught Beverley Allitt, retired Det Supt Stuart Clifton, was heavily cited as the former Brexit Secretary said cops had failed over the case.

Sir David said: “Justice demands that the police look at everyone. It does not permit them to fix on one individual and build a case around them.”

The MP compared Letby’s case to another involving the same force – that of solicitor Sally Clark, who was jailed for killing her two infant sons in 1999 on bogus medical testimony before being freed on appeal four years later.

But he claimed despite the “clear warning signs” of that experience, Cheshire Police had “broken the rules, disregarded all relevant safeguards, and committed clear and serious departures from statutory guidance” during Operation Hummingbird – its probe into Letby.

He described how Letby’s case had been reviewed by two experienced former police officers.

Dr Steve Watts, an ex-Assistant Chief Constable, who authored the National Police Guidelines on the investigation of deaths in healthcare settings, and retired Detective Superintendent Stuart Clifton, the cop who brought ‘Angel of Death’ Beverley Allitt to justice after she murdered three tots at Grantham and Kesteven Hospital in 1991.



Sir David said they initially thought Letby was guilty – but both now “believed she was the victim of a serious miscarriage of justice after examining the hard facts”.

He told MPs how he had analysed the case using “proper police procedure, prosecutorial standards, medical murder investigation guidelines, CPS guidance, and the evidence emerging from the Thirlwall public inquiry”, as well as the assistance of Dr Watts and Mr Clifton.

Starting with the neonatal unit at the Countess of Chester Hospital, where Letby is accused of carrying out her crimes, Sir David said it was “failing”, and that its medical management was “at best inadequate, and at worst, appalling”.

He pointed out that on the day Letby was suspended, the unit was downgraded and prevented from taking any more seriously ill babies, and other reviews of the hospital had found no evidence of criminal activity.

This included one from the Royal College of Paediatricians which identified “numerous failures of medical care”.



But, Sir David said, Cheshire Police ignored this – and the jury in Letby’s trial was never told.

He added the unit had no specialist neonatologists, or neonatal nurses, a 22 percent staffing shortfall, multiple outbreaks of infection, and sewage dripping from the ceiling, as well as 12 stillbirths at the time Letby was accused of attacking babies – all of which was also ignored by detectives.

However, Sir David said despite this, the young nurse was identified as the “focus of suspicion” following a single meeting police had with two consultants – Dr Stephen Brearey, and Dr Ravi Jayaram – and Operation Hummingbird “was up and running within 24 hours”.

He claimed that the medics themselves had demonstrated “poor care” in the past, including fatal incidents involving babies, and added that one had “misled” jurors by saying he’d “virtually caught” Letby during one of the murders – which was later contradicted by his own emails.

Sir David said: “These doctors themselves could very well have contributed to the spikes in deaths attributed to Letby.



“Dr Watts poses an important question that poses an answer: ‘Where was the decision not to treat the doctors as suspects, or the other nurses, or the cleaner?’”

He added that according to the Criminal Procedure and Investigations Act, Cheshire Police was required to pursue all reasonable lines of inquiry – meaning bosses “didn’t follow either the letter of the law, or best professional practice”.



He also said the local Cheshire CPS made all the charging decisions in the case.

Guidance said the fact the deaths took place in a healthcare setting meant it should have been referred to the CPS’ Serious Crime Division based in London – and the failure to do so meant that “proper safeguards and specialist scrutiny were never implemented”.

To make matters worse, Sir David claimed cops then failed to listen to ‘explicit’ guidelines from the National Crime Agency.

This advised them to appoint a panel of specialists including forensic and neonatal pathologists, a toxicologist, a nurse with experience of special baby units, and statistical experts – and even sent a list of names to assist.

But he added Cheshire Police ‘blatantly ignored this’, and instead built its case around “one so-called expert” – Dr Dewi Evans, who he said “ran a business” providing medical testimony in court which had earned him at least £80,000, and probably far more.



In a scathing assessment of Dr Evans, Sir David described him as a retired paediatric consultant, and not a specialist neonatologist, who had boasted of never losing a case – which he said was the language of someone who “tailors his evidence to suit the prosecution’s case”.

He told how a senior judge with previous knowledge of Dr Evans had written to Letby’s trial judge to describe his evidence as “worthless”, adding “he makes no evidence to provide a balanced opinion”. But again, this wasn’t shown to jurors.

Sir David said Dr Evans even told Cheshire Police it wasn’t necessary for them to seek out other opinions when they asked him if they should consult the NCA guidance, and was ultimately appointed as both an advisor and expert witness in the case – something Stuart Clifton said was a “clear conflict of interest”.

And he added “alarm bells should have been ringing” when Dr Evans declared that he “knew instantly that something suspicious had gone on” after reading the Letby case notes for just 10 minutes.

Other witnesses who eventually gave evidence had GMC investigations and patient complaints against them, said Sir David – which he said was proof Cheshire Police had “failed to do its due diligence”.



Turning to one of the key pieces of evidence against Letby – an ‘infamous’ shift table which showed she was on duty during all the incidents where babies either collapsed or died – Sir David said some deaths that took place when the nurse WASN’T at the hospital hadn’t been featured.

He added: “Cheshire Police, who compiled the table, chose only to highlight the shifts during which Letby was present, while disregarding similar events when she was not.

“The seven deaths ultimately charged as murders were, in effect, selected because she just so happened to be on duty. As Stuart Clifton bluntly puts it, ‘Police cherry-picked rota cases to match Letby’s shifts’.”

Incredibly, Sir David said a leading statistics expert, Professor Jane Hutton, DID warn senior officers the “whole approach was wrong” when one approached her for help – but was told prosecutors had instructed the force “not to pursue this avenue any further”.

Once more, Sir David said jurors weren’t informed – which was “unsurprising really, because this completely destroyed the prosecution’s statistical case – the foundation of their entire argument”.



He told Parliament Professor Hutton now believed the statistical errors were even worse than in the case of Sally Clark.

Sir David said he’d written to the Chief Constable of Cheshire Police to ask how the “bogus statistics” had been compiled – but he’d refused to answer any questions or take part in any further discussion.

And he said significant investigations like Operation Hummingbird should be independently reviewed by detectives from other forces, but this didn’t take place.

Summing up his speech, Sir David said: “They failed to pursue alternative lines of inquiry.

“They failed to refer the case to the appropriate specialist bodies, including the Serious Crime Division.



“They failed to follow established guidance in their appointment of expert witnesses.

“They failed to engage with complex statistical evidence, thus failing to correctly inform the jury.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“On the evidence before us, there is reason to believe that the law may have been broken. At the very least, there have been clear and serious departures from statutory guidance.

“In the meantime, I shall be writing to the Director of Public Prosecutions about these issues.”

News.title: NHS doctor ‘who celebrated October 7 horror’ is charged with supporting Hamas and stirring up racial hatred
News.content:
TERROR PROBE NHS doctor ‘who celebrated October 7 horror’ is charged with supporting Hamas and stirring up racial hatred

AN NHS doctor has been charged with supporting Hamas and stirring up racial hatred.

Rahmeh Aladwan, 31, has been charged with “multiple counts of inviting support for Hamas”, the Metropolitan Police said.





The doctor, of Pilning, South Gloucestershire, was cuffed at her home this morning for breaching bail conditions from a previous arrest.

She has been charged with using words “that were threatening, abusive or insulting intending thereby to stir up racial hatred” on July 21 last year.

Aladwan is also accused of publishing or distributing “written material that was threatening, abusive or insulting intending thereby to stir up racial hatred” on November 19 last year.

On July 23, August 23, October 7 and December 31, the NHS worker allegedly invited support for proscribed organisation Hamas.

The charges of inviting support for a proscribed organisation relate to comments or other material posted online.

Aladwan has been remanded in custody and will appear at Westminster Magistrates’ Court tomorrow.

The doctor has also ranted about the Holocaust being a “concept”.

She marked the second anniversary of Hamas’ attack on Israel with social media posts appearing to glorify “the day ‘Israel’ was humiliated”.



Hamas murdered more than 1,200 people, raping women and slaughtering children, and took a further 251 hostages in their horrific attack on Israel on October 7.

Aladwan said “glory to the breaking of the 17-year-long illegal siege”, complete with a photo of a bulldozer ploughing through a fence on Oct 7.

And at a pro-Palestine protest, she said: “For me, Palestine is the entirety of the map. I have my opinion.”

The case has triggered major alarm over patient safety in the NHS – particularly for Jewish patients.

She is also being investigated by the General Medical Council over antisemitic social media posts about the Royal Free Hospital in London.



More to follow… For the latest news on this story keep checking back at The Sun Online

Thesun.co.uk is your go-to destination for the best celebrity news, real-life stories, jaw-dropping pictures and must-see video.

Like us on Facebook at www.facebook.com/thesun and follow us from our main Twitter account at @TheSun.

News.title: Set for Life winning numbers LIVE: National Lottery results for March 26 with huge jackpot
News.content:
One lucky player could take home tonight’s Set For Life jackpot, totalling £3.6 million.

The winning numbers for the National Lottery's Set For Life draw are 14, 17, 26, 31, 37, and the Life Ball is 4. The jackpot pays £10,000 a month for 30 years. Players enter the draw by choosing five numbers from 1 to 47 and one Life Ball from 1 to 10. To win the top prize, a player must match all five numbers and the Life Ball. Matching the five numbers without the Life Ball wins the second-tier prize: £10,000 a month for one year.

Set For Life is drawn twice a week on Mondays and Thursdays at 8pm. We’ll bring you the results as they come in.

News.title: Major Madeleine McCann search update just weeks before 19th anniversary
News.content:
There has been a major update regarding the search for Madeleine McCann - nearly 19 years after she went missing.

Home Office ministers approved the request to extend funding for Operation Grange, which was launched in 2011 after Madeleine McCann disappeared from a holiday apartment in Praia da Luz, Portugal, in May 2007.

The specialist Metropolitan Police team leading the investigation has been allocated £86,000 for 2026/27, down from £108,000 the previous year. The inquiry has cost around £13.3m since it was first launched.

Madeleine was on holiday with her family in Praia da Luz in the Algarve, Portugal, in 2007 when she vanished aged just three years old. The 19th anniversary of her disappearance will fall on May 3.

Image: PA) PA)

Three police officers and one member of staff working on a part-time basis now make up Operation Grange. DC Mark Draycott, a member of the team, told the trial of convicted rapist Christian Brueckner that he had taken a call from a man called Helge Busching who had named Brueckner as a suspect in the Madeleine McCann case.

Brueckner was released after serving a prison sentence in his native Germany last September for the rape of an American woman, then 72 years old, in Portugal in 2005. He remains a prime suspect in Madeleine's disappearance although he has never been charged with any crimes in connection with it.

Image: Phil Harris) Phil Harris)

Meanwhile, in January Kate and Gerry McCann shared a heartfelt statement saying they hope the New Year would bring "the breakthrough we long for".

Posting to the Official Find Madeleine Campaign Facebook page, the pair said: "As 2025 draws to a close, we wanted to take the opportunity to thank everyone who has supported us, in whatever way, over the past year & for continuing to hope for positive news of Madeleine."

The McCanns thanked the public and police for their support throughout the investigation, and ended their message with: "With our best wishes for a peaceful & positive new year & may 2026 bring us the breakthrough we long for. Kate, Gerry & family."

News.title: Gang rape victim Noelia Castillo Ramos dies by euthanasia after battling courts and family
News.content:
Noelia Castillo Ramos has died by euthanasia after struggling with mental health issues and being left paralysed following a horrific gang rape.

The 25-year-old from Barcelona died this evening after the procedure, which she had requested, was allowed to go ahead.

Noelia's dad had attempted to block the euthanasia via the backing of the right-wing Catholic campaign group Christian Lawyers.

Christian Lawyers, the organisation which has been representing Noelia’s dad, said in a statement released around 7pm UK time: “Noelia has already been euthanised.

“At Christian Lawyers, we deeply regret her death and denounce that this case highlights the serious flaws in the euthanasia law, which does not protect the most vulnerable people. We urge politicians to use her story to drive urgent changes and prevent something like this from happening again.

“Thanks to everyone who has empathised with the family during these very difficult times. You can understand that the parents are broken after years of trying to support her in her rehabilitation.”

The first two jabs induced deep sedation and the third most potent one caused cardiac arrest.

Noelia’s mum Yolanda Ramos spent last night with her at Sant Camil Hospital before the start of the scheduled 6pm (5pm GMT) procedure, which is designed to “ensure a death without suffering.”

Image: Yas) Yas)

Her father Geronimo Castillo, who waged a lengthy legal fight to try to get judges to block his daughter’s request, was among other relatives including her siblings and maternal grandmother who arrived at the hospital to spend time with her earlier today.

Noelia, who spoke openly about her decision to end the mental and physical pain she has suffered for years during a Spanish TV interview earlier this week, was alone in the room when she died at her own request apart from the doctor administering her injections.

Regional health sources also confirmed Noelia’s death.



Before she was euthanised in a procedure which had been scheduled for 6pm local time, the 25-year-old is said to have asked her family to spend extra time with her.



They were due to leave her alone with a medic who administered her three injections at around 5.30pm but are understood to have stayed for at least an extra half-hour.



The scheduled start of the procedure is believed to have begun around 6.30pm local time (5.30pm GMT).

She was alone in the room when she died, at her own request, apart from the doctor administering her injections.

Image: Tik Tok) Tik Tok)

'I can't take the pain anymore'

Noelia, from Barcelona, had endured years of mental health issues after being gang raped by three boys at a facility for vulnerable people in 2022. She later jumped from a fifth-floor window in October that year but survived.

She suffered irreversible spinal injuries due to the fall and was unable to move from the waist down. Noelia said she lived with "severe neuropathic pain and incontinence".

The young woman said her dad told her she "had no heart" after revealing she wanted to die via euthanasia. She has since lashed out at her dad's attempts to block the procedure, claiming he had been largely absent since mounting a legal challenge which saw a previous assisted suicide appointment blocked.

"He never calls or writes to me," Noelia told programme Y ahora Sonsoles (And Now Sonsoles), hosted by Sonsoles Onega. "Why does he want me alive, to keep me in a hospital."

In the interview, Noelia added: "I hope I can finally rest because I can't take this family anymore, I can't take the pain anymore, I can't take everything that torments me from what I've been through."

Noelia's euthanasia plea was upheld by the Catalan Guarantee and Evaluation Commission (CGEC), despite her father's protests. She had originally been scheduled to undergo the procedure in August 2024.

Assisted dying has been legal in Spain since 2021. Before her daughter's death, Yolanda said: "Not all parents are prepared for this."

Noelia, who said she would undergo the procedure today, claimed she wanted to be alone at the moment of her death. She added: "I don’t want anyone inside, I don’t want them to see me close my eyes."

She had been living in a nursing home where she was treated for her injuries and claimed to be living with constant physical pain and psychological suffering. Noelia had claimed to have been neglected by her parents and was put in a foster care facility by the Catalan government when she was 13.

She recounted multiple instances of abuse at the facility, including several cases of sexual assault. The last instance was at the hands of the three boys.

Noelia said her mental health problems began after her parents divorced when she was a child, and she was already receiving psychiatric treatment aged 13. She told Spanish media she had a borderline personality disorder, and obsessive compulsivity.

For mental health support, contact the Samaritans on 116 123, email them at jo@samaritans.org or visit samaritans.org to find your nearest branch.

News.title: 'Racism makes my blood boil' – five reasons to join the march against the Far Right on Saturday
News.content:
History teacher Joseph Emmett will take a bus from his home on the Welsh borders this weekend and travel to London for the biggest demonstration against the Far-Right Britain may ever have seen.

His reasons come straight from the lessons he teaches his secondary school students. “When I teach about Hitler and the Nazis, children always ask, ‘how did it happen?’” the 49-year-old National Education Union rep says. “And the answer is, ‘because he wasn’t stopped’.” This Saturday, the Together Alliance will stage a major national demonstration in London, with the aim of standing up for unity and building public awareness about the growing threat of the far right in the UK.

The alliance of trade unionists and environmentalists, community activists and faith leaders, musicians, athletes, entertainers, MPs and councillors, is backed by a growing list of well-known names from Paloma Faith to Paul Weller, Lenny Henry to Leigh-Anne Pinnock, Christopher Eccleston to Charlotte Church, and Steve Coogan to Juliet Stevenson. It’s also backed by actor David Harewood, former footballer Shaka Hislop and singer Beverley Knight.

“We stand for love over hate, hope over fear and unity over division,” Lenny Henry said in a statement. “We’re coming together against racism, join us and be a part of it.” Paloma Faith added: “There is no world that I want to live in where discrimination is acceptable for anything. No race, gender or class is superior to any other…this is why racism makes my blood boil.”

‌



View 4 Images Joseph Emmett will join the demonstration against the Far-Right Britain (Image: Supplied)

More than 500 supporting organisations include the Trades Union Congress, Friends of the Earth, Show Racism the Red Card, UNISON, Unite the Union, Love Music Hate Racism, Stand Up to Racism, HOPE not Hate, Stop the War, Fire Brigades Union, Muslim Council of Britain and many more.

Some of the reason behind the demo has been the scale of far-right protests over the last year. Rather than be forced into ‘counter-demonstrations’, organisers say they want to hold a positive event that shows Britain as it really is. Police estimated that between 110,000 and 150,000 people attended the so-called ‘Unite the Kingdom’ rally in central London in September, where Tommy Robinson and Elon Musk addressed the crowd.

"For the first time the Far Right are driving politics at a national level, and Far Right protests on our streets are mobilising the biggest numbers they ever have," organisers say. “Their false promises seize on the very real economic problems people face, in order to scapegoat migrants, Muslims, and refugees. But we know we can change things when we stand together.”

‌



Charity worker Bilal Hussain, 32, is travelling to London on one of many coaches chartered from Nottingham. “Decades ago, there was a spate of hate crime, including an incident where a Muslim woman had her hijab pulled off,” says Bilal, the deputy director of Himmah. “There was a huge effort to make the city safer, but since the pandemic we have seen a massive increase in Far Right rhetoric and a sharp increase in hate crimes in Nottingham.

View 4 Images Bilal Hussain, deputy director at Himmah charity in Nottingham (Image: Supplied)

“It’s gone up particularly in the city centre. Black and brown people are being targeted. With the onset of social media, hate is being internationalised and spread. I’ll be on the march because it’s not about doing it alone, it’s about building in partnership, showing solidarity. In Nottingham, we've poured such time and care into working with friends and neighbours across the city to build an open, tolerant place that brings out the best of us. We don't want the good work undone.”

Law student Funton Ajijola, 20, a member of Amnesty’s Student Action Network Committee, is travelling down from Reading. “When you’re on social media you see a lot of Far Right content being published, being pushed. I’m compelled to go to the march because I want to make it clear that ‘I’m here too’.

‌



“We’re live here – and we can’t let people push a different narrative and pretend that we don’t exist. They spread misinformation and then this leaks into politics. Why should I sit there and let people do that? The march is a way to show we are a community and that we’re not going to allow people to be divided and demonised.”

As well as people travelling to London from across the UK, we also spoke to Londoners who see the march as a defence of their city. The capital has been under sustained attack from the Far Right on social media because of its proud multicultural identity and the Muslim religion of its popular mayor Sadiq Khan.

View 4 Images Tommy Robinson at the Unite The Kingdom rally in September 2025 (Image: Getty Images)

Dom Eagleton, a 54-year-old charity worker, will be performing at the demo with his London-based Swing Dance group. “One of the reasons I’m taking part is as a response to the awful march in September which was led by Tommy Robinson – whose mum is an Irish migrant,” he says. “This is a great opportunity to respond and show we are bigger and better than them in every sense of the word.

‌



“Swing Dance came from oppression, segregation and racism in America. Imagine a UK without the influence of black culture? That’s what the Far Right want, calling for ICE-style agents. We love our diversity, our culture, our city and we’ll be making that clear. It doesn’t matter about your faith, skin colour – it’s a joy to dance and a joy to watch and we want to bring some of that to the day. For many people it will be their first time attending an event like this. It’s a family-friendly celebration of everything that’s great about this country. Some of my family will be there, including my 83-year-old step-dad.”

Londoner Fargol Ayan, 25, is an events organiser, originally from Iran. She will be also be attending with the Swing Dance group. “We have to show up for one another, to show what kind of society we are,” she says. “I want to be part of that collective voice. To show that the Far Right narrative is not the one – to show that when we come together in person it is beautiful. I live in London – a brilliant example of a multicultural community. We all come from everywhere, we share our culture, our food, our ideas and that is our strength.”

Back in Wales, history teacher Joseph is preparing for his journey to London. “The Oxford English Dictionary word of the year last year was ‘ragebait’ and the algorithms are there to monetise it,” he says. “We have to push back. We are taking back the narrative, being honest about who we are and the kind of country we are. Celebrate our diversity – our strength. If we keep attacking each other we know where it leads, we’ve seen the pattern. We have to stand together in solidarity.”

For all the details on the day go to www.togetheralliance.org.uk/march

News.title: UK coffee company collapses into liquidation as director issues notice
News.content:
UK coffee company collapses into liquidation as director issues notice

Instant Coffee Supplies Ltd confirmed via a notice of liquidation that it is closing down - the British coffee supplier recorded more than £1m in capital last year

View 2 Images A British coffee supplier is closing down (stock) (Image: Getty)

A British coffee supplier has confirmed it is closing down.

‌



The director of Instant Coffee Supplies Ltd, Trevor Geoffrey Blashfield, 81, confirmed the move via a notice of liquidation in The Gazette. "Notice is hereby given that the following resolutions were passed on 24 March 2026, as a Special Resolution and an Ordinary Resolution respectively: That the Company be wound up voluntarily," the notice read.

‌



Cullen & Co UK has been appointed as the liquidator to oversee the process of winding up. The company reported more than £1.08 million in capital and assets of £1.4 million in 2025. The winding up of a company is the formal legal process of selling its assets, paying creditors and dissolving the legal entity behind the company name. It appears the company is going into Members’ Voluntary Liquidation (MLV).

‌



View 2 Images The company announced it was closing down via a liquidation notice in The Gazette (stock) (Image: Getty)

Major UK travel company liquidation means all holidays cancelled and no money back READ MORE :

Major airline falls into administration after 33 years – all flights cancelled READ MORE :

As per a statement issued by law firm Baker McKenzie, a Members' Voluntary Liquidation is a formal, tax-efficient process to close a solvent company, initiated by shareholders, when the business is no longer needed.

‌



The firm's restructuring and insolvency experts explain that an MLV involves appointing a liquidator to distribute assets, pay any outstanding liabilities and wind up the company, usually allowing distributions to be treated as capital gains, Express reports.

Common reasons a company may enter an MVL include:

Retirement: A business owner decides to retire and has no successor

Company no longer needed: A company has fulfilled its purpose, such as a special-purpose vehicle for a specific project

Restructuring: A corporate group wishes to reduce the number of entities in its structure

Capital distribution: Shareholders wish to distribute accumulated profits in a tax-efficient way

It comes as fashion retailer River Island has shut down a total of 32 stores this year as part of a restructuring plan in the wake of a £32.3million pre-tax loss.

Article continues below

The company's CEO, Ben Lewis, said that the firm has "a large portfolio of stores no longer aligned to our customers' needs".

The rise of online shopping and escalating operating costs were cited as the primary challenges by the retailer. River Island, which has 200 outlets nationwide and employs over 5,000 people, continues to operate its remaining UK and Ireland branches after closing 32 sites, with no additional closures confirmed at present.

News.title: Defence chiefs sign off hundreds of new homes for troops in £9billion investment
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Defence chiefs sign off hundreds of new homes for troops in £9billion investment

Facing war in the Middle East and the risk of the Ukraine conflict spilling over into the rest of Europe the biggest overhaul of troops' housing is underway with a £9bn 'new homes for the military' boost

View 4 Images Defence Secretary John Healey visits London miltary housing

Defence chiefs have signed off hundreds of new homes for troops and thousands of accommodation refurbishments in a sweeping £9bn property boost. It represents the most significant transformation of UK military housing in over 50 years, with more than 40,000 service family homes to be modernised, refurbished or rebuilt.

‌



As many as 265 new houses, apartments and bungalows will be built at RAF Brize Norton - the first new Ministry of Defence houses since 2017 and building will start within weeks. The massive build-for-troops project is aimed at undoing years of brutal Tory defence cuts in which soldiers, sailors and RAF personnel suffered appalling drops in living standards.

‌



View 4 Images Defence Secretary John Healey in Cyprus (Image: PA)

‌



Russian military insiders admit it will take Putin 100 years to take Ukraine READ MORE :

And it comes as increasingly British troops from all three services are facing the prospect of a higher tempo towards possible war and having to defend UK interests at home and abroad. Wars in the Middle East and Ukraine have sparked a major overhaul of defence spending and putting troops in harm's way.

The new Brize Norton homes follow the government’s landmark deal to bring 36,000 homes back into public ownership. That deal has allowed an overhaul of Armed Forces housing and is saving the taxpayer £600,000 per day in rent no longer needing to be paid.

Within the next year at least 2,000 military properties will be modernised, in a bid to improve conditions in which our armed forces personnel live with families or alone. Defence Secretary John Healey told the Mirror exclusively: “At a time of greater global uncertainty, when we’re asking more of our Forces and their families, a decent home has never mattered more.

‌



“Housing is at the heart of all of our lives. And after years of neglect, the new development at Brize Norton demonstrates delivery on our overhaul of military housing. I am determined that British military families will get homes they can be proud of, as we renew our nation’s contract with those who serve.”

View 4 Images Troops board HMS Dragon before Middle East duty (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Thousands more service family homes will be revamped as part of a decade-long transformation to allow troops facing increasing risk of war to have a good home. The UK MoD said the project was to allow “British personnel working round the clock to defend the nation get the housing they deserve.”

‌



Local Oxfordshire council planners have given the green light to the scheme for RAF Brize Norton which will see a range of flats, houses and bungalows built on land at Carterton near the long-standing air base. Building will start in the coming weeks.

View 4 Images Iranian oil depot burns after Israeli strikes (Image: IRIB via AP)

The MoD told the Mirror: “Ministers are determined to deliver at pace as British personnel and their families continue to make extraordinary sacrifices to keep Britain safe – including working round the clock on the crisis in the Middle East. Better housing is a key part of this Government’s promise to renew the nation’s commitment to those who serve, and a key factor in fixing Armed Forces recruitment and retention.

Article continues below

“This work is a step towards widened access for families to which a military home was not previously available, and 1,000 such families will be given the keys to a defence house this year.” The government will also introduce an “interim rental allowance” which will accelerate widened access for those military families whose needs cannot be met in a particular location.

Natalie Elphicke-Ross OBE, Chair of the Defence Housing Strategy Review team said: “Making sure that military families have a warm, safe, modern, comfortable home is central to our plan for a generational change to military housing.” The work is underpinned by the largest sustained increase in defence spending since the end of the Cold War, hitting 2.6% of GDP from 2027, and the UK defence budget will total £270 billion across this Parliament alone.

News.title: Benefit cheat who claimed she was housebound with anxiety caught ziplining in Mexico
News.content:
Benefit cheat who claimed she was housebound with anxiety caught ziplining in Mexico

Catherine Wieland, 33, was caught surfing and ziplining on holiday after claiming tens of thousands of pounds in benefits for being 'housebound' with anxiety

View 4 Images Catherine Wieland, 33, fraudulently claimed more than £23,000 in benefits (Image: PA)

A benefits cheat who claimed she was 'housebound' with anxiety was caught after she was found to have gone surfing and ziplining on holiday in Mexico.

‌



Catherine Wieland, 33, claimed she suffered anxiety so crippling she could barely leave her home - but the Department for Work and Pensions said it discovered she had been lying about her condition.

‌



The DWP said it found evidence that Wieland, from Goring-by-Sea, West Sussex, had been surfing in Cancun and went on trips to Thorpe Park three times, all while racking up over £23,000 in Personal Independence Payments (Pip) over more than two years.

‌



She also spent money on manicures, tanning sessions and trips to a private Harley Street dentist.

View 4 Images Wieland was found to have gone on an adventure holiday in Mexico despite claiming she was 'housebound' with anxiety (Image: PA)

Motability sends letter to users today over changes as new tax means average £400 rise in payments READ MORE :

DWP issues statement over major tax change affecting pension funds READ MORE :

But when investigators confronted her with bank statements she told them: "I didn't realise you're not allowed to leave your house", the DWP said.

‌



While claiming her health was so poor she could not cook or wash herself, Wieland made 76 beauty appointments, visited 60 pubs, clubs and restaurants and splashed out in foreign currencies.

View 4 Images The 33-year-old claimed her anxiety had got worse after returning from holiday (Image: PA)

After returning home from her luxury trip to Mexico, Wieland submitted a review claiming her condition had got worse.

‌



She pleaded guilty to failing to notify a change of circumstances and must now repay the £23,662 stolen from taxpayers between 2021 and 2024.

Wieland was sentenced on Thursday to 28 weeks in custody, suspended for 18 months, the DWP said.

View 4 Images She was handed a suspended sentence after a DWP investigation brought her to court (Image: PA)

‌



Andrew Western, a minister in the DWP, said: "This is an insult to every hardworking taxpayer and to people who genuinely depend on Pip.

"Wieland lied repeatedly, milked the system for every penny she could get and then had the nerve to claim her condition was worsening while she was ziplining and surfing in Mexico.

"We are committed to finding those who try to defraud taxpayers, and they will face the consequences."

Article continues below

In the financial year ending 2025, the total cost of benefit overpayments in Great Britain due to fraud and error was estimated at £9.5 billion - amount to 3.3% of all spending on benefits.

Under new powers granted with the Public Authorities Act in December, DWP can now take money directly from bank accounts or strip benefits fraudsters of their driving licence if they refuse to repay money claimed illegally.

News.title: Artemis 2 launch dates and times revealed – full list of seven dates in April
News.content:
Artemis 2 launch dates and times revealed – full list of seven dates in April

NASA’s Artemis 2 mission could lift off in just a few days' time - with the historic flight marking the first time humans have visited the Moon since 1972

Artemis II - Nasa's mission to the moon

The first lunar mission since 1972 is days away from launch, as NASA's Artemis 2 rocket is on the platform at the Kennedy Space Centre in Florida.

‌



The historic 10-day mission will see four astronauts circle the Moon for the first time in over half a century, with the launch set to take place in as little as six days’ time.

‌



NASA’s Artemis 2 mission could lift off on April 1, marking a massive step in the space program’s long-term plan to eventually build a base on the Moon inhabited by astronauts. The mission was originally planned for February but after problems during testing, the launch has been pushed back to a six-day launch window in April.

‌



View 3 Images NASA's Space Launch System rocket with the Orion spacecraft is at Launch Complex 39B (Image: AP)

Inside Artemis 2 as NASA's manned mission to Moon is days away from blasting off READ MORE :

Major Artemis change as NASA announces astonishing $20bn moon base plan READ MORE :

Ground teams at Launch Complex-39B are now working to ready the 98m tall (322ft) moon rocket for its launch in the coming days.

The four astronauts who make up the crew of the Artemis 2, Commander Reid Wiseman, pilot Victor Glover, Christina Koch and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen went into quarantine for 14 days on March 18 to make sure they are fighting fit for the April launch.

‌



Why can Artemis 2 only launch on a few days?

The launch will see the Orion spacecraft, which sits atop the massive rocket and houses the crew, first delivered into high earth orbit, 45,000 miles above the ground.

View 3 Images The Orion capsule sits atop the massive rocket (Image: CRISTOBAL HERRERA-ULASHKEVICH/EPA/Shutterstock)

NASA Artemis 2 launch date as delays to historic moon mission explained READ MORE :

NASA Artemis 2 astronauts quarantined as update given on historic moon launch READ MORE :

"The launch day and time must allow SLS to be able to deliver Orion" to that orbit, NASA officials wrote in an Artemis 2 explainer last month. "Orion also must be in the proper alignment with the Earth and moon at the time of the trans-lunar injection burn," they added.

‌



Timing is crucial for the mission to work properly, the space agency said. "The trajectory for a given day must ensure Orion is not in darkness for more than 90 minutes at a time so that the solar array wings can receive and convert sunlight to electricity, and the spacecraft can maintain an optimal temperature range... Mission planners eliminate potential launch dates that would send Orion into extended eclipses during the flight."

View 3 Images The four crew are in final preparations for launch (Image: Getty)

NASA Artemis 2 launch set for April as leaders highlight mission risks READ MORE :

Viable launch windows also have to take into account Orion's return to Earth, which must allow for a specific entry profile.

Article continues below

The first attempt for launch Artemis 2 will take place at 6.24pm EDT on April 1 (11.24pm GMT). However bad weather or the need to replenish commodities such as rocket fuel can push the launch back. There are six other dates, five at the start and one at the end of April, which NASA have published.

The seven possible Artemis 2 launch dates:

News.content: The OECD's global growth forecast for this year is unchanged at 2.9%, but it predicts inflation across the G20 countries will be 4%, sharply up from its previous forecast of 2.8%. The G20 includes the EU plus 19 other countries, collectively accounting for 85% of the world's economic output.
